_comment: soar_quarantine_alert v2 — fires on three distinct detection paths
_comment_paths: 2. zeek.notice: Scan::Port_Scan notice fired by Zeek's scan detection policy
3. zeek.ssh: 5+ failed auth attempts from the same source IP in the last 1 min

script.source: // Prefer the top document hit (IOC or port-scan path); fall back to the top SSH brute-force IP if hits are zero. def ip = 'unknown'; def mac = ''; def dataset = 'unknown'; if (ctx.payload.hits.total.value > 0) { def src = ctx.payload.hits.hits[0]._source; if (src.containsKey('source')) { if (src.source.containsKey('ip'))  { ip  = src.source.ip; } if (src.source.containsKey('mac')) { mac = src.source.mac; } } if (src.containsKey('event') && src.event.containsKey('dataset')) { dataset = src.event.dataset; } } else { def buckets = ctx.payload.aggregations.ssh_brute_force_candidates.by_source_ip.buckets; if (buckets.size() > 0) { ip = buckets[0].key; dataset = 'zeek.ssh (brute-force)'; } } ctx.payload.soar_ip = ip; ctx.payload.soar_mac = mac; ctx.payload.soar_dataset = dataset; return ctx.payload;

_comment_action: webhook_to_soar_agent (retired WS-B): posted here unsigned. agent_app.py's
/alert route requires an HMAC-SHA256 signature (HMAC_HEADER/HMAC_TS_HEADER,
see agent_app.py:717-726) and Watcher's webhook action has no built-in way
to compute one, so every delivery on this path was rejected outright once
the agent's fail-closed signature gate went in.
The IOC-match path is now PARTIALLY covered by the signed Logstash SOAR
trigger instead (scripts/setup/configs/logstash/logstash.conf, Category
5 — an HTTP output that HMAC-signs the request body with
SOC_AGENT_HMAC_SECRET before POSTing to /alert), but NOT equivalently:
Logstash's CRITICAL_THREAT tag is driven by threat_intel.yml, which is
IP-indexed only, while this watcher's IOC path also matches on
threat.indicator.domain (Zeek's own domain-intel framework). A
domain-only indicator hit with no corresponding bad IP in
threat_intel.yml sets neither path off. Closing that gap means adding
domain-keyed lookups to the Logstash translate stage — not done here.
The Scan::Port_Scan and SSH-brute-force paths this watcher also covers
watcher also covers are windowed aggregations over a rolling window;
Logstash's per-event filter/output model has no equivalent, so those two
paths remain detection-only here — visible in Kibana's Watcher history,
not auto-dispatched to the agent. Do not restore the webhook action
without adding real HMAC signing (e.g. a Watcher script/transform that
computes sha256=HMAC(secret, ts + '.' + body) matching agent_app.py's
_signed_payload() scheme).

logging.text: CRITICAL_THREAT candidate: dataset={{ctx.payload.soar_dataset}} source.ip={{ctx.payload.soar_ip}} source.mac={{ctx.payload.soar_mac}}. IOC-match alerts are dispatched to the SOC agent via the signed Logstash trigger; this watcher is now log-only for Kibana visibility and for the port-scan/SSH-brute-force paths — see _comment_action.
